# NotebookTools

> NotebookTools is a free Chrome extension that imports YouTube videos, X (Twitter) posts, webpages, and selected text directly into Google NotebookLM. Right-click any highlight to add it.

NotebookTools helps researchers capture web content into NotebookLM without copy-paste. Uses your existing Google sign-in.

## What it does

- **YouTube**: Import button on watch pages only. One click to add the current video.
- **X / Twitter**: Import threads and articles as text sources.
- **Webpages**: Import any page URL from the extension popup.
- **Selected text**: Highlight text, right-click, choose **Add to NotebookLM**.

## Key facts

- **Price**: Free
- **Platform**: Google Chrome (Manifest V3)
- **Auth**: Your signed-in NotebookLM session
- **Affiliation**: Independent — not affiliated with Google
- **Chrome Web Store**: https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/notebooktools-%E2%80%94-import-to/fhjgbniefbimnklnnomlhkkiekcjddmj

## Common questions

**How do I summarize a YouTube video in NotebookLM?**
Add the video as a source, then ask for a summary in the notebook chat. Use NotebookTools to import from the watch page in one click. Full guide: https://www.notebooktools.com/blog/summarize-youtube-videos-notebooklm

**What is NotebookTools?**
A free Chrome extension that imports YouTube, X, webpages, and highlighted text into NotebookLM. Details: https://www.notebooktools.com/blog/what-is-notebooktools

**How do I add highlighted text to NotebookLM?**
Select text on any page, right-click, choose Add to NotebookLM, pick a notebook.

**How do I import a YouTube video?**
Visit a watch page, click the NotebookLM button, choose a notebook. Or paste the URL in NotebookLM manually.

**NotebookLM vs Gemini — which is better?**
NotebookLM is source-grounded research; Gemini is general-purpose AI. Comparison: https://www.notebooktools.com/blog/notebooklm-vs-gemini

**NotebookLM vs ChatGPT — what's the difference?**
NotebookLM cites your uploaded sources; ChatGPT is broader but less grounded. Comparison: https://www.notebooktools.com/blog/notebooklm-vs-chatgpt

**What are the best NotebookLM alternatives?**
Perplexity, Mem.ai, Notion AI, Elicit, and others — see: https://www.notebooktools.com/blog/best-notebooklm-alternatives

**How many sources can NotebookLM hold?**
Up to 50 sources per notebook. Full guide: https://www.notebooktools.com/blog/notebooklm-source-limits

**Where can I get help?**
Support page: https://www.notebooktools.com/support
Email: rafeequemavoor@gmail.com
